# Requirements Quality Checklist: Register User Account Use Case

**Purpose**: Validate completeness, clarity, and consistency of the requirements.
**Created**: 2026-02-08
**Feature**: /home/aiden/ECE_493_Lab_2/specs/001-register-user-account/spec.md

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all core user actions in the primary flow covered by explicit functional requirements? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all listed functional requirements testable and scoped to the feature? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for each acceptance scenario in the spec? [Coverage, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK004 Are there explicit requirements corresponding to each documented edge case? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is the requirement text for FR-001 free of ambiguous verbs or outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the requirement text for FR-002 free of ambiguous verbs or outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK007 Are any referenced rules or limits quantified with explicit criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [ ] CHK008 Are password rules explicitly defined (length, character classes)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Do acceptance scenarios align with functional requirements without missing outcomes? [Consistency, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [ ] CHK010 Do success criteria align with the scope described in functional requirements? [Consistency, Spec §Functional Requirements, Spec §Measurable Outcomes]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK011 Are all success criteria measurable and objective? [Measurability, Spec §Measurable Outcomes]
- [ ] CHK012 Do success criteria avoid implementation-specific language? [Measurability, Spec §Measurable Outcomes]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK013 Are primary, alternate, and exception flows all defined in requirements or scenarios? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [ ] CHK014 Are exception scenarios covered for each failure type listed in the use case? [Coverage, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK015 Are edge cases written as concrete, testable requirements rather than questions? [Ambiguity, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK016 Are boundary conditions defined for inputs and limits referenced by edge cases? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK017 Are email validation rules (format, uniqueness) explicitly defined and measurable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK018 Are performance expectations specified in the requirements (not just plans)? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK019 Are accessibility requirements specified for user-facing interactions? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK020 Are privacy or data handling requirements specified for sensitive data? [Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK021 Are all assumptions tracked with a resolution path or source of truth? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK022 Are dependencies on external artifacts explicitly mapped (use cases, scenarios, acceptance tests)? [Traceability, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK023 Are role terms used consistently across requirements and scenarios? [Ambiguity, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [ ] CHK024 Are there any conflicts between acceptance scenarios and functional requirements? [Conflict, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §Functional Requirements]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
